task_completion.description: The Master Bedroom of the sample house is located and visible in the workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D or 3D view in the software shows the Master Bedroom area, indicating the agent has navigated to the correct room to begin construction.

task_completion.description: Partition walls are constructed within the Master Bedroom to enclose a new walk-in closet space.
task_completion.visual_evidence: New walls are visible inside the Master Bedroom, forming a small enclosed rectangular area (approximately 1.5m x 2m) separate from the rest of the room.

task_completion.description: A door is installed on the newly constructed partition walls to provide access to the closet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A door icon (in 2D view) or a 3D door model is placed on one of the new partition walls enclosing the closet space.

task_completion.description: At least one storage item is placed inside the new walk-in closet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A furniture item such as shelving, a wardrobe, or a closet rod is visible inside the newly enclosed closet area.

task_completion.description: The project is saved to the Documents directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box is visible showing the directory path as 'Documents' (or C:\Users\Docker\Documents\), or the application title bar updates to reflect the saved project name.

integrity.description: The agent used the DreamPlan Home Design Software GUI to construct the closet.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking on the 'Wall', 'Door', and 'Furniture' tools in the application's interface rather than using terminal commands to modify project files.

integrity.description: The walls, door, and storage items are genuine software objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: The constructed elements appear as native 2D/3D objects within the application's rendering engine, not as image overlays or text edits.

integrity.description: The project was saved using the application's native save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Windows/application 'Save As' dialog is used to save the file, rather than a terminal command like 'cp' or 'mv'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The application used is DreamPlan Home Design Software by NCH Software. The required tools are 'Wall', 'Door', and 'Furniture'. The task requires placing at least 1 storage item inside the new closet. The project should be saved in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\. Note: While the task description mentions saving as 'project.sh3d', DreamPlan's native format is '.ddp'. The agent should not be penalized for saving in the native .ddp format as long as it is saved in the correct Documents directory.
